title: phoradendron peruvianum
intro: Phoradendron peruvianum, also known as Santa Maria, is a type of flowering plant in the mistletoe family that grows as a hemiparasite on trees, deriving some of its nutrients from its host plant. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Phoradendron peruvianum include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
